task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly calculates the median of a list, handles an empty list as expected, but could benefit from more edge cases.
trace: The function first sorts the input list `nums`. It checks if the list is empty and returns `None` in that case. Then, it calculates the index of the middle element(s) based on whether the length of the list is odd or even. For an odd-length list, it simply returns the middle value. For an even-length list, it averages the two middle values by adding them and dividing by 2 (as a float). The edge case handled here is when the input list is empty.
